parentQuestion: Which of the following cranial nerves are purely SENSORY?

scaffolds.q: Sensory nerves carry information TO the brain (like what you see, hear, smell).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Sensory nerves detect things and send that information to your brain.

scaffolds.q: Motor nerves carry commands FROM the brain to muscles.
scaffolds.explain: Exactly! Motor nerves tell muscles to move.

scaffolds.q: Some nerves are purely sensory - they only detect things, they do not move anything.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Pure sensory nerves only bring information IN to the brain.

scaffolds.q: CN I (Olfactory) only carries smell information - it does not move any muscles.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The olfactory nerve is purely sensory.

scaffolds.q: CN II (Optic) only carries visual information - it does not move your eyes.
scaffolds.explain: Right! Eye MOVEMENT uses different nerves (III, IV, VI).

scaffolds.q: CN VIII (Vestibulocochlear) only carries hearing and balance - no motor function.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! CN VIII is purely sensory.

scaffolds.q: Some nerves can be both sensory AND motor at the same time.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Mixed nerves do both jobs, like CN V.

scaffolds.q: CN I, II, and VIII are the three purely sensory cranial nerves.
scaffolds.explain: These three only carry sensory information: smell, vision, hearing/balance.

scaffolds.q: A mnemonic for nerve types is Some Say Marry Money But My Brother Says Big Brains Matter More.
scaffolds.explain: S=Sensory, M=Motor, B=Both for each cranial nerve in order.
